title: XML operations should not be vulnerable to injection attacks
description: XML injections occur when an application builds an XML-formatted string from user input without prior validation or sanitation. In such a case, a tainted user-controlled value can tamper with the XML string content. Especially, unexpected arbitrary elements and attributes can be inserted in the corresponding XML description.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: XML injection vulnerability: User input is directly concatenated into XML string without proper encoding.

never_trigger_on: Code using proper XML APIs (XDocument, XElement)
logic.reasoning: Detects direct string concatenation or interpolation of user input into XML strings. This can lead to XML injection attacks. Safe alternatives include using SecurityElement.Escape(), XDocument/XElement APIs, or proper encoding.

should_not_trigger: var doc = new XDocument(new XElement("user", new XElement("name", userInput)));
